title: Reis sexuality and role in the series Split
display_title: Reis sexuality and role in the series Split
wikitext:
[]: Rei's sexuality & role in the series (Split)             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 03, 2009 5:30 am Allemann wrote: Xard wrote: Not to mention it just doesn't fit in Rei's character and characterisation.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Aug 03, 2009 8:10 pm Excellent post. I couldn’t have said it better myself.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:04 pm I don't see how you can expect to argue such a point backwards from the symbolism you're trying to project on it when that symbolism is in part dependent upon the point of contention. Doubly so when the symbolism in question is more of a western monotheistic motif, and one specifically tied to the religious themes which we already know are mostly inapplicable. I agree with the conclusions, but not at all with the argument.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:15 pm @Thread: Quick answer is NO. Long answer is that this would be one of those particularly pointless examples of overanalysis in an attempt to argue something that's never suggested and doesn't really have any bearing on characters, narrative, or theme and may, as Xard pointed out, even contradict them. NemZ wrote: I don't see how you can expect to argue such a point backwards from the symbolism you're trying to project on it when that symbolism is in part dependent upon the point of contention. Doubly so when the symbolism in question is more of a western monotheistic motif, and one specifically tied to the religious themes which we already know are mostly inapplicable.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:22 pm So you didn't notice the virgin mary namedropping, obviously the connection he was trying to make the whole time? Yes the theme is older than christianity, but that's all part of motheistic base. Virgin mothers don't tend to crop up in pantheist systems. My point is it's circular. He's arguing that Rei can't have been molested because that would violate the symbolic role he wants to see in her... which he sees in her in part because of his perception that she's inviolable.

[]:             Quote Post by thefeelingofikarishinji » Mon Aug 03, 2009 9:29 pm Well, Gendo wouldn't have had to actually have sex with the vessel that contained Rei's soul; he had those giant tanks of laughing, flirty, naked Rei clones ostensibly for the 'dummy plugs' which it seems Ritsuko might have guessed could have had another use for Gendo...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 10:00 pm NemZ wrote: So you didn't notice the virgin mary namedropping, obviously the connection he was trying to make the whole time?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Aug 03, 2009 10:18 pm If that wasn't his point he shouldn't have mentioned it right at the beginning of the post, as it definately colors everything that follows. By Xard's own explanation the 'untouchable' nature is very much open to interpretation, so It may be far more to do with a sense of disconnection from others rather than some sort of divine sanctity. Besides, Rei is clearly not asexual... her entire transcendence sequence is very sexual in nature, so trying to force her into a virginal goddess frame seems ill-fated. Yes she's a form of goddess, but a primal one rather than some idealized maiden. And despite this, like I said earlier, I still agree that Gendo and Rei never got it on. We are arguing about the form of the argument, not the answer.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Aug 03, 2009 10:42 pm NemZ wrote: If that wasn't his point he shouldn't have mentioned it right at the beginning of the post, as it definately colors everything that follows.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 04, 2009 12:26 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: the second paragraph...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 2:00 am NemZ wrote: The first paragraph was a single throw-away sentence. Big whoop.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 4:11 am NemZ wrote: I don't see how you can expect to argue such a point backwards from the symbolism you're trying to project on it when that symbolism is in part dependent upon the point of contention. Doubly so when the symbolism in question is more of a western monotheistic motif, and one specifically tied to the religious themes which we already know are mostly inapplicable. I agree with the conclusions, but not at all with the argument.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 5:17 am Hey mods, since this thread is no longer about Gendo possibly boinking Rei and is now about Rei's symbolic significance/Virgin Mary/Goddess stuff, do you think we can get a split from This Post onward?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 5:30 am oh god, no - if we're going to have Rei thread this is quite insufficient opening for it :S well whatever...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 5:37 am Xard wrote: oh god, no - if we're going to have Rei thread this is quite insufficient opening for it :S well whatever...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 5:54 am Oh fine fine, that was just hyperbole. I just figured out that IF we're going to have "Rei Ayanami - in depth" topic it would have to be ginormous topic, more essay or article or... I mean, we've just concentrated on one aspect of her character in context of her relationships with other people *shrug* But really, I'm fine if this gets split. But I hope it is more specific topic then than whole "Rei Ayanami" case.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 04, 2009 12:06 pm Oh fine, after all that effort I can't just ignore you... but this is definately becoming split-worthy if we keep this up. Xard wrote: I didn't try to make any connection.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 5:48 pm NemZ wrote: If Rei and Lilith are the same being, how can they be separate parts of the triad? Where does unit 01 fit in, and should it listed as separate from Yui as well?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Aug 05, 2009 8:54 pm Split. Keep it civil.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Aug 05, 2009 11:02 pm Umm... I'm not sure the title is quite right. I don't have a better suggestion at the moment though. Eva Yojimbo wrote: Rei and Lilith aren't the same being.
